content.full_text:
Fred Balcom 
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Strt1et • Suite :ooo • Sacraml•nto, CA 9,s811 
( 9 I 6) ; 2 2 -S 6 6 0 • Fa X ( 9 I 6) :12 2 -U 8 8 6 
September 11, 2017 
647 Bluebird Place 
Davis, CA 95616 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-17-214 
Dear Mr. Balcom: 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the "revolving door" provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
Since your questions at this time are general in nature, we are providing informal assistance. 
Infonnal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or 
formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation l 8329(c)(3 ).) 
Please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby ( 1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. In addition, the Fair Political Practices Commission does not provide advice 
relating to past conduct. This letter should not be construed as assistance on any conduct that may 
have already taken place. 
QUESTION 
As the former Director of Special Education within the California Department of Education 
who, as a part of your duties, oversaw the suspension of certification of non-public schools, does 
the Act's permanent ban prevent you from providing expert testimony in a civil suit brought against 
one of these schools by a former student? 
CONCLUSION 
Generally, where the State of California is not a party to, nor does it have a direct and 
substantial interest in, the proceedings, and all the parties are private entities, the permanent ban 
would not prohibit you from testifying in court as a paid expert witness. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014 . All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unle.Xs otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practice!'l 
Commission are contained in Sections l81 I0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No.1-17-214 
Page No. 2 
You are the fonner Director of Special Education within the California Department of 
Education ("DOE") before retiring in December 2015. During your employment, you were 
responsible for direct supervision of a manager and unit that conducted certification reviews, and 
certified Non-Public Schools and Agencies ("NPS/A"). As part of this certification process DOE 
investigated alleged violations of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act, California 
Pcluc1ti011 C0dc. Űcc!inn ,04 0f!hc Rchnbilitntion .t\ct, and the contractual obligations cstabliűhcd 
with Local Educational Agencies ("LEA"s) that placed students into NPS/As. 
This unit, acting on behalf of the DOE, has authority to suspend or revoke certification of 
the NPS/ A and to order corrective actions in order to reinstate certification. This authority includes 
ordering corrective actions upon LEAs that failed to properly ensure that students placed into a 
NPS/ A by the LEA were properly served and to ensure that all rights and protections are afforded to 
those students. 
You note that there were multiple instances where the DOE unit investigated allegations of 
violations of these various legal provisions and suspended the license of a specific NPS/A and 
ordered corrective actions on the NPS/A and the LEA. 
ANALYSIS 
Permanent Ban 
The "permanent ban" prohibits a former state employee from "switching sides" and 
participating, for compensation, in certain proceeding involving the State of California and other 
specific parties, or assisting in the proceeding if the proceeding is one in which the fonner state 
employee participated while employed by the state. (Sections 8740 I and 87402; Regulation 
18741. I.) Section 87401 states: 
"No fonner state administrative official, after the termination of his or her 
employment or term of office, shall for compensation act as agent or attorney for, 
or otherwise represent, any other person (other than the State of California) before 
any court or state administrative agency or any officer or employee thereof by 
making any fonnal or infonnal appearance, or by making any oral or written 
communication with the intent to influence, in connection with any judicial, 
quasi-judicial or other proceeding if both of the following apply: 
"(a) The State of California is a party or has a direct and substantial interest. 
"(b) The proceeding is one in which the fonner state administrative official participated." 
The permanent ban also prohibits a fonner governmental employer from "aiding, advising, 
counseling, consulting, or assisting in representing" any other person in any proceeding in which 
the fonner governmental employee would be prohibited from participating. (Section 87402.) 


File No. l-17-21 4 
Page No. 3 
Definitions. The pennanent ban applies when an official has pennanently left or takes a 
leave of absence from any particular office or employment. (Regulation 18741.1 (a)(l ).) "'Judicial, 
quasi-judicial or other proceeding' means any proceeding, application, request for a ruling or other 
detennination, contract, claim, controversy, investigation, charge, accusation, arrest or other 
particular matter involving a specific party or parties in any court or state administrative 
agency . ... " (Section 87400(c).) An official is considered to have "participated" in a proceeding if 
he or she took part in the proceeding "personally, and substantially through decision, approval, 
dis:ipprov.il. fonn1! ,,,rittm recommendation, rendering advice on n substantial basis, inve,tigation. 
or use of confidential infonnation .. .. " (Section 87400(d).) 
You supervised the manager and unit responsible for NPS/ A certification and de­
certification proceedings at the DOE. You note that you participated in at least one decision to 
suspend certification of a NPS/A. Under the prohibitions of Sections 87401 and 87402, you would 
be banned pennanently from representing or appearing for any person/or compensation before any 
court or state administrative agency with the intent to influence, in connection with any judicial or 
quasi-judicial proceeding, if (I) the State of California is a party or has a direct and substantial 
interest; and (2) the proceeding is one in which you participated. The dctenninations as to ( 1) 
whether the state is a party or has a direct and substantial interest; and (2) your involvement in a 
proceeding will depend on the specific facts involved in each matter. 
Generally, in proceedings where the State of California is not a party, does not have a direct 
and substantial interest, and all the parties are private entities, the pennanent ban would not prohibit 
you from testifying as a paid expert witness. Because we do not have enough facts to analyze the 
specific future instances where you may be presented with an opportunity to provide testimony as 
an expert witness, we cannot detennine whether the pennanent ban of Sections 87401 and 87402 
would apply to you in these cases now. lfthere are other proceedings to which the pennanent ban 
might apply, you will need to analyze them based on the infonnation above, or contact us for 
further infonnation. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (91 6) 322-5660. 
By: 
ZWN:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Jack Woodside 
General Counsel 
: 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
